sport_context.nutrition_strategy: High protein; strict carb cycling around training blocks
sport_context.notes: Lower back: no heavy deadlifts, hyperextensions, loaded good mornings. Knees: controlled ROM on deep knee flexion — max 90 degrees on all squatting patterns.

constraints.lower_back: Moderate injury. No RDLs, no conventional deadlifts, no hyperextensions, no loaded good mornings. Hip hinge work limited to cable pull-throughs and machine/barbell hip thrusts (padded, supported). Back rows must be supported (cable or machine) — no heavy free-standing bent-over rows.
constraints.knees: Mild pain. Controlled ROM only — no ATG squats, no deep knee flexion beyond 90 degrees. Monitor all quad-dominant movements.

progression.rule: When all working sets are completed at RPE 7 or below for two consecutive sessions, increase load by the smallest available increment (2.5 kg for barbells, 2 kg for dumbbells). Never exceed RPE 9 in the accumulation block.

weekly_schedule.thursday: Push B — Shoulders (Press) + Chest Accessory + Triceps
weekly_schedule.friday: Pull B / Posterior Chain — Back + Glutes + Hamstrings

warmup_template.general: 5 min light cardio (bike or rowing machine — no treadmill running due to lower back protocol). Follow with 3–5 targeted mobility drills for the day's primary joints.
mobility_wednesday: Leg swing lateral 10 reps per side

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Eat M1 (pre-workout) 30–45 min before gym start.

metabolic_primer.name: Tabata Upper Body Blast
metabolic_primer.protocol: 20s work / 10s rest × 8 rounds alternating between Push-Up and Dumbbell Shoulder Press (5–8 kg)
metabolic_primer.instructions: Alternate each round: Round 1 push-ups, Round 2 DB shoulder press, repeat. Use knee push-ups if standard is too taxing at this point in the cut. Goal is cardiovascular activation and shoulder pre-activation — not maximal load. Rest 90s between full Tabata cycles if needed. No jumping.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press

warmup_sets.note: Movement pattern rehearsal — slow and controlled
Feel the chest stretch at the bottom
Build intent for the working sets

exercises.note: Lower bar to mid-chest, not the neck. Drive shoulders into the bench throughout the press. 2s eccentric — do not drop the bar. If any shoulder impingement discomfort, substitute with Dumbbell Bench Press.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Press
exercises.note: 30-degree incline only — avoid steep inclines that stress the shoulder joint. 2s eccentric. Control the path: elbows at 45 degrees to the torso, not flared.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Set the cable pulley low. Lead with the elbow, not the wrist. Stop at shoulder height — do not raise above the head. Slight forward lean is acceptable. Perform both sides: 3 sets per side.

exercises.name: Cable Tricep Pushdown (Rope Attachment)
exercises.note: Keep elbows pinned at sides throughout. Flare the rope handles at the bottom of each rep and pause briefly to maximize tricep contraction. Do not lean forward to add momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Overhead Tricep Extension (Seated)
exercises.note: Seated to eliminate lumbar load. Elbows point straight up. Lower the dumbbell behind the head with a 3s eccentric — the overhead position maximizes long-head stretch. Keep core braced.

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Full upper back and biceps volume day.

metabolic_primer.name: AMRAP Posterior Shoulder Circuit
metabolic_primer.protocol: 3-exercise circuit — complete as many rounds as possible in 7 minutes: 10 × Band Pull-Apart + 10 × Cable Face Pull (light) + 10 × Machine Row (very light load)
metabolic_primer.instructions: Move with intent and short transitions. This primes the posterior shoulder and upper back before loading. Scale machine row weight to something that feels like RPE 5 — this is activation, not a working set. No explosive movements or lower body involvement.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)

warmup_sets.note: Focus on lat depression before pulling
Build lat engagement — feel the stretch at the top

exercises.note: Initiate every rep by depressing the scapulae (pull shoulder blades down) before the arms move. Pull to upper chest. 2s concentric, 2s eccentric. Never pull behind the neck.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (Close Grip)
exercises.note: Brace the core before each rep. Slight lean back at the contraction is fine — do NOT hyperextend the lumbar spine (lower back protocol). Drive elbows back and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the peak. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row (Supported on Bench)
exercises.note: Bracing arm and knee on the bench removes lumbar load entirely — critical for lower back protocol. Keep hips squared (do not rotate to swing the weight). Drive elbow high and back. 2s eccentric. Perform both sides.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl (Alternating)
exercises.note: Supinate the wrist fully at the top of every rep to maximize bicep peak contraction. Do not swing — elbows stay pinned at sides. Full extension at the bottom on every rep.

exercises.name: Cable Hammer Curl
exercises.note: Neutral (hammer) grip throughout. Targets the brachialis, which lies under the biceps and adds arm thickness. Keep elbows pinned. Great complement to the supinated dumbbell curl.

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Knee protocol active: max 90-degree knee flexion on all squat patterns. Lower back protocol: no deadlifts or RDLs.

metabolic_primer.name: Glute and Hip Activation Circuit
metabolic_primer.protocol: 3-exercise circuit — as many rounds as possible in 8 min: 15 × Glute Bridge (bodyweight) + 10 × Banded Clamshell per side + 10 × Bodyweight Squat (controlled, 90-degree ROM only)
metabolic_primer.instructions: Focus on mind-muscle connection during the glute bridge — feel the glutes contracting, not the lower back. Banded clamshell activates hip abductors which protect the knee joint during loaded work. Bodyweight squat depth is hard-capped at 90 degrees (knee protocol). No jumping at any point.

exercises.name: Leg Press

warmup_sets.note: Confirm 90-degree stop point before adding load

exercises.note: Feet hip-width, mid-platform. Lower to 90-degree knee angle — do not push deeper (knee protocol). Keep lower back pressed firmly into the pad throughout. Do not lock out the knees at the top of the press.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.note: 90-degree ROM hard cap. Feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Drive through the full foot — do not shift onto the toes. The machine guides the movement but still brace the core deliberately. If knee discomfort, substitute with Bulgarian Split Squat using bodyweight or light dumbbells.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.note: 2s concentric, 3s eccentric. Full extension at the top — do not hyperextend. The seated position keeps the lumbar spine out of the movement. This is pure quad isolation — do not rush the reps.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.note: Plantarflex the foot (point toes down) to optimize hamstring recruitment. 2s eccentric. Do not let the hips rise off the pad — hips rising means the lower back is compensating. Lower back protocol: the lying position is safe as long as hips stay neutral.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust

warmup_sets.note: Use a barbell pad. Confirm neutral spine at the top before loading.

exercises.note: Upper back on a flat bench. Drive through the heel. Squeeze the glutes hard at the top and hold for 1 second. Critical: the spine does NOT extend at the top — ribs stay down, spine stays neutral. Use a barbell pad to protect the hip bones. Machine hip thrust is preferred over barbell for lower back safety.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.note: Full stretch at the bottom (slow controlled descent), explosive drive up, hold 1s at the top. Calves respond well to high reps and time under tension. Vary foot position across sets: toes neutral for 2 sets, toes slightly out for 1 set, toes slightly in for 1 set.

sessions.focus: Push B — Shoulders (Press) + Chest Accessory + Triceps
sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Shoulder press is the primary strength movement today — treat it with the same focus as bench press on Monday.

metabolic_primer.name: Tabata Band Shoulder Circuit
metabolic_primer.protocol: 20s work / 10s rest × 8 rounds alternating between Resistance Band Overhead Press and Resistance Band Lateral Raise
metabolic_primer.instructions: Use a light resistance band — this is shoulder pre-activation, not a working set. Move continuously with good tempo. No jumping or lower body explosive involvement. The goal is blood flow and shoulder joint preparation before the seated press.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press

warmup_sets.note: Confirm full ROM before adding load

exercises.note: Seated position eliminates lumbar load. Lower dumbbells to ear level, press to just short of lockout. Do not lean back excessively — keep torso upright. This is the primary strength lift for Thursday — treat it with the same focus as bench press on Monday.

exercises.name: Cable Fly (Low to High)
exercises.note: Set pulleys at the lowest position. Arc the arms upward and together as if hugging a tree from below. This vector targets the upper pec (clavicular head) — complements Monday's flat barbell press. Controlled throughout — no jerking at the start of the movement.

exercises.name: Pec Deck Fly
exercises.note: Pure chest isolation. Keep the movement in the chest — shoulders should not be the primary driver. If the pec deck is unavailable, substitute with Dumbbell Fly (flat or incline). Controlled eccentric — resist the pads opening.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Strict form — zero momentum. Use 3/1/3 tempo: 3s raise, 1s hold at the top, 3s lower. This maximizes time under tension on the medial delt. Thursday carries the highest shoulder volume of the week — this is intentional. Keep the weight light enough to complete all reps with this tempo.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Skull Crusher
exercises.note: Lower the bar toward the forehead (stopping 1 cm above). Elbows directly above the shoulders — do not let them flare. The long head of the tricep is the primary target. Keep wrists neutral throughout. A slight backward lean of the bar on the eccentric gives extra long-head stretch.

exercises.name: Tricep Dip (Assisted Machine)
exercises.note: Use the assisted dip machine (preferred) for shoulder safety. If unavailable, use a bench dip. Lean slightly forward to bias the triceps over the chest. Monitor shoulder for internal rotation stress — if discomfort, substitute with Tricep Pushdown.

sessions.focus: Pull B / Posterior Chain — Back + Glutes + Hamstrings
sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Lower back protocol fully active. All back work is supported or cable-based. Hip hinge work via cable pull-through and hip thrust only.

metabolic_primer.name: AMRAP Posterior Chain Activation
metabolic_primer.protocol: 2-exercise circuit — as many rounds as possible in 7 min: 10 × Cable Pull-Through (light) + 10 × Resistance Band Good Morning (band only — NOT a barbell)
metabolic_primer.instructions: Cable pull-through teaches the hip hinge pattern without spinal compression — keep it light and focus on pushing the hips back, feeling the hamstring stretch, then driving forward through the glutes. Band good mornings are activation only — use the lightest band available. This primes the posterior chain (glutes and hamstrings) for the session. No barbell, no spinal loading.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Underhand Grip)
exercises.note: Underhand (supinated) grip shifts emphasis to the lower lats and adds bicep involvement — different stimulus from Monday's wide-grip pulldown. Lean back slightly (30 degrees) and pull to the upper chest. Avoid craning the neck forward to meet the bar.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Machine Row
exercises.note: Chest pad means the lower back is completely unloaded — this is the preferred row variation for Carol's lower back condition. Drive the elbows back (not down). Squeeze and hold 1 second at the peak contraction. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.note: Attach a rope to the low pulley. Stand facing away from the machine, straddle the cable. Hinge at the hip — push the hips back until you feel hamstring tension, then drive the hips forward and squeeze the glutes. Zero spinal compression — this is the ideal hip hinge for Carol's lower back. Do not hyperextend at the top.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.note: Seated leg curl (vs lying on Wednesday) hits the hamstring at a longer muscle length, targeting a different portion of the strength curve — deliberate variation. 2s concentric, 3s eccentric. Keep the hips against the pad.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Second hip thrust session of the week — glute training frequency is doubled intentionally. Female athletes respond well to higher glute frequency. Same form cues: drive through the heel, neutral spine at the top, 1s squeeze at peak. Use machine hip thrust or barbell with pad.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
exercises.note: Friday closes the week with light bicep volume. Neutral grip (hammer). Can be done alternating or bilateral — choose based on how the arms feel at the end of the session. The brachialis work here complements the supinated curl from Tuesday.
